skill.name: twilio-communications
skill.description: Build communication features with Twilio: SMS messaging, voice calls, WhatsApp Business API, and user verification (2FA). Covers the full spectrum from simple notifications to complex IVR systems and multi-channel authentication. Critical focus on compliance, rate limits, and error handling. Use when: twilio, send SMS, text message, voice call, phone verification.
skill.summary: Implement Twilio SMS, Verify, WhatsApp, voice, and IVR communication patterns with compliance and error handling guidance.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static shell-execution, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance findings are false positives caused by markdown code fences and communications terminology. The real risk is medium because the skill provides examples that read Twilio credentials from environment variables and perform real SMS, Verify, and voice workflows through Twilio services.

medium_findings.title: Twilio Credential Environment Access
medium_findings.description: The examples read TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID, TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN, TWILIO_PHONE_NUMBER, TWILIO_VERIFY_SID, SALES_PHONE, and SUPPORT_PHONE from environment variables. This is a normal secret-handling pattern, but generated applications must protect these values and avoid logging or exposing them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The credential and phone number environment accesses are directly visible in the examples. The pattern is legitimate for Twilio integrations, so the concern is operational exposure rather than malicious intent.

medium_findings.title: Examples Trigger Real Communication Workflows
medium_findings.description: The examples create Twilio SMS messages, Verify checks, and voice call routing. These operations can contact real users, create cost, and require consent, rate limiting, and compliance controls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Twilio SDK calls and voice routing destinations are explicit. The behavior matches the skill purpose, but it has real-world security, privacy, and billing impact.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick findings point to markdown code fence delimiters around Python examples. I did not find evidence of executable shell commands in the skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are markdown delimiters, not shell execution syntax in runnable code. The repository contains only SKILL.md, so no companion script changes that interpretation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a Communications-Term False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are associated with 2FA and OTP wording, including guidance to avoid storing OTP codes. I did not find evidence of weak hash or encryption implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe authentication use cases and state that Twilio handles OTP codes. No cryptographic algorithm or custom credential hashing is present.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Request and Status Handling
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings map to Verify service selection, verification status, and invalid webhook request handling. These are normal Twilio integration details, not host or network discovery.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line context shows application status and request validation handling. I did not find commands or API calls that enumerate the local system or network.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential-Backed Twilio SDK Usage
dangerous_patterns.description: The examples combine Twilio SDK usage with environment-provided account credentials. This is expected for the domain, but misuse can expose secrets or send unauthorized communications.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The credential reads and SDK operations are visible in the code examples. The pattern is legitimate but sensitive enough to require warning and review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Webhook Security Depends on Signature Validation
dangerous_patterns.description: The IVR example validates Twilio request signatures before handling call routes. This is a safe pattern, but generated variants must preserve it to prevent forged webhook requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The validation logic is explicit and beneficial. The risk is conditional because removing or weakening this pattern would expose voice workflows to forged requests.

content.user_title: Build Twilio Messaging and Voice Workflows
content.value_statement: Twilio projects need careful handling of credentials, compliance, retries, and delivery status. This skill gives implementation patterns for SMS, Verify, WhatsApp, voice, and IVR workflows.
actual_capabilities: Explains SMS sending with E.164 phone validation and delivery status callbacks.
Shows Twilio Verify patterns for phone verification, 2FA, and multi-channel delivery.
Covers TwiML IVR flows with Gather, Dial, Record, Redirect, and webhook validation.
Highlights Twilio-specific sharp edges such as opt-out tracking, A2P 10DLC, and rate limits.
Uses environment variables for Twilio account, auth token, service, and phone number configuration.
limitations: The skill is guidance only and does not include a complete runnable application.
Some code examples are truncated and need completion before production use.
It does not manage Twilio account setup, billing, carrier registration, or consent records.
Users must verify current Twilio limits, compliance rules, and API behavior before deployment.

use_cases.title: Transactional SMS Notifications
use_cases.description: Add order confirmations, shipping updates, reminders, or account alerts with validation and delivery handling.

use_cases.title: Account Verification and 2FA
use_cases.description: Use Twilio Verify for signup checks, login challenges, password resets, and high-value transaction confirmation.

use_cases.title: Voice IVR Routing
use_cases.description: Design phone menus that collect keypad input, route callers, validate Twilio webhooks, and handle voicemail.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Basic SMS
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a transactional notification.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Twilio communications skill to draft a simple SMS notification flow. Include E.164 validation, environment variables, and delivery status handling.

prompt_templates.title: Add Phone Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Twilio communications skill to design a phone verification flow with Twilio Verify. Include send, check, retry, and rate-limit behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Production Messaging System
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Twilio communications skill to review a production SMS design. Focus on consent, opt-out storage, A2P 10DLC, error handling, and cost controls.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Secure IVR
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Twilio communications skill to design an IVR webhook flow. Include signature validation, menu routing, session state, failure paths, and audit logging.

output_examples.input: Create an SMS notification pattern for appointment reminders.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan with phone validation, message segmentation warnings, status callbacks, retry policy, and opt-out handling.

output_examples.input: Review my Twilio Verify login flow for security gaps.
output_examples.output: A review covering channel choice, retry limits, code checking, account lockouts, fraud signals, and safe storage of Twilio credentials.

output_examples.input: Design a phone menu for sales, support, and voicemail.
output_examples.output: An IVR route map with Twilio signature validation, keypad collection, fallback behavior, call transfer destinations, and voicemail handling.

best_practices: Keep Twilio credentials in secret storage or environment variables, never in source code.
Validate webhook signatures before processing calls, messages, or delivery status updates.
Track consent, opt-outs, rate limits, retries, and carrier registration before sending production traffic.
anti_patterns: Do not send SMS or WhatsApp messages without user consent and opt-out handling.
Do not build custom OTP storage when Twilio Verify can manage codes and expiration.
Do not remove signature validation from voice, message, or status callback webhooks.

faq.question: Does this skill send messages by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation guidance and examples. Your application performs the Twilio API calls.

faq.question: What Twilio products does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers SMS, Verify, WhatsApp Business API concepts, voice calls, TwiML, and IVR patterns.

faq.question: Can I use it for production systems?
faq.answer: Yes, but treat the examples as patterns. Complete the code, add tests, and verify Twilio compliance requirements.

faq.question: How should credentials be handled?
faq.answer: Store account SIDs, auth tokens, service SIDs, and phone numbers outside source code. Use managed secrets where possible.

faq.question: Does it include compliance guidance?
faq.answer: It highlights consent, opt-out tracking, A2P 10DLC, rate limits, and signature validation. Legal review may still be required.

faq.question: Are the examples complete?
faq.answer: No. Some examples are truncated in the source and must be completed before use.
